label: About
title: About The Charcoal Spiral Observatory
short_blurb: A daily generated front page where the image is the interface.
body: Daily Frontpage is a daily generated interactive front page. Each edition starts from recent saved signals and source research, becomes a new image-led scene, and stays explorable in the archive. The image is the interface: visible marks, objects, and surfaces in the scene open the real sources that shaped the day.

This iteration began with 16 recent saved-signal files and 12 captured links, then narrowed the page to 9 source windows. A source image from f4.bcbits.com helped set the palette and material direction. The selected sources included Image 1983-1998 (Skintone Edition), by Susumu Yokota and @supertommy: promised to ship before the movie so 53 days 731, plus 7 more, and the scene became The Charcoal Spiral Observatory: A nocturnal observatory reduced to a charcoal field: one dominant matte-black celestial oculus floats high in the frame, leaving broad empty darkness around it. Below center, a warm vellum square translates the Yokota cover’s centered pale panel, heavy black surrounding typography, soft grain, and delicate curling violet-brown line into a quiet star-map fragment. A single oxidized copper spiral arc becomes the second secondary form, crossing the vellum like a handmade orbit. Keep the palette to charcoal pigment, warm vellum, and dark copper, with smoked-glass glints. Visible but small: IMAGE 1983-1998 on the vellum edge, Galaxy DNA etched into the copper curl, Under a Glass Bell as a tiny clear aperture, Taaritt as a sand notch, Fabric as a dim acid-green dial, Circuits as a reed scratch, cam.eee as a profile moon, open world as a low horizon chip, and 13-26 as a date fleck. Silent, spacious, optical. The first plate read used fallback geometry, then the mask audit tightened the regions against the finished image. Review artifacts are in tmp/daily-process-runs/daily-process-2026-04-27T15-03-28-995Z.

typography.rationale: Bricolage Grotesque and Space Grotesk fit record, arcade, and listening-room editions while Fraunces keeps source captions tactile.
